---
title: "SISAP 2026 Indexing Challenge"
---

## Overview

The **SISAP 2026 Indexing Challenge** benchmarks approximate nearest-neighbor and
sparse retrieval algorithms at scale. Participants submit a GitHub repository
containing a Docker-based solution; the evaluation pipeline runs each solution
on the official datasets and records build time, query time, and recall.

Three tasks are evaluated:

| Task | Description | Dataset(s) | Required recall |
|------|-------------|------------|-----------------|
| **Task 1** | All-*k*-NN on dense embeddings (*k* = 15) | gooaq-small, wikipedia-small | ≥ 0.80 |
| **Task 2** | *k*-NN query search on dense embeddings (*k* = 30) | llama-dev | ≥ 0.80 |
| **Task 3** | *k*-NN query search on sparse embeddings (*k* = 30) | fiqa-dev, nq | ≥ 0.90 |

The **throughput** metric is:

- **Task 1**: total vectors ÷ (build time + query time)
- **Tasks 2 & 3**: number of queries ÷ query time

See the [Leaderboard](leaderboard.qmd) for current results and the [Plots](plots.qmd) page for recall/QPS Pareto frontiers.
